patch.value: GH-507: recovery suggested_retry appends <value> to missing boolean EXTENSION flags (contract arity ignored)

When an extension command throws a structured CommandError whose remediation names a declared boolean flag (SDK command contract declares --allow-empty with no value_name), pm correctly identifies the missing flag but renders it value-taking in recovery.suggested_retry: '--allow-empty "<value>"'. Expected bare '--allow-empty'. The flag renders correctly as boolean in help and contract metadata — only recovery suggestion generation ignores the extension contract's arity. This is the extension-contract sibling of GH-500/pm-6y58 (FIXED via PR#502 for built-in flag tables): the arity fix does not consult SDK/extension command contracts. Observed with pm-cli 2026.7.10 + pm-todos 'todos sync'.

suggested_retry consults the active extension/SDK command contract for flag arity; boolean extension flags render bare (--allow-empty) while value-taking extension flags keep the placeholder; repro from GH-507 (todos sync guard failure) yields a suggestion that succeeds verbatim; regression test covers boolean + value extension flags; GH-507 closed with a comment linking the fix

patch.value: Suggested retries consult active extension command contracts for flag arity.
Boolean extension flags are bare; value-taking flags retain placeholders.
Recovery distinguishes boolean and typed value flags and resolves the actual invoked command occurrence.
Implemented extension-aware recovery flag arity and command-token fidelity.

author: codex-root
message: Reopen for final review edge cases: repeated trailing command tokens and required untyped flag arity.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Final-review implementation start: Gemini identified command-token repetition after the invoked path; Greptile reproduced required:true untyped flags as value-taking. Both belong to the existing recovery arity lineage, so pm-9qcr alone is active.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Final-review fix evidence: descriptor lookup now evaluates every command-token occurrence and selects the longest registered path, handling both leading global values and trailing option/positional values. required:true untyped extension flags now remain value-taking in suggested retries, matching Commander registration. Focused suite 122/122; full suite 5533/5533; exact coverage 100/100/100/100; lint, zero duplication, static quality, token budget, and typecheck passed.

patch.value: Trailing/leading token collisions resolve the canonical descriptor, boolean flags stay bare, and required untyped flags receive value placeholders.
Recovery suggestions remain executable when command tokens repeat before or after invocation and when required flags omit explicit type metadata.
Suggested retries use longest registered command-path matching across all token occurrences and honor required flag arity.
Resolved final review edge cases for repeated command tokens and required untyped flag arity.
